raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The section advances a specific contribution claim (RDL, multi-task formulation) and a gap statement (majority-vote bias); a general opinion request plausibly invites evaluation of whether that framing is accurate and well-scoped.","R1.2":"The section builds a causal chain—non-negligible reader disagreement → majority-vote bias → RDL remedy → improved AUC and ECE—whose internal logical coherence is a natural target of any open-ended critical opinion.","R1.3":"Quantitative performance claims are asserted (AUC 0.872, ECE 0.039 vs. baselines on CSAW-CC) against a training set (DDSM-Plus) different from the evaluation set; evidence-claim alignment is central to assessing whether those numbers actually support the stated conclusions.","R1.4":"The paper explicitly differentiates RDL from symmetric label smoothing and prior disagreement-aware methods; assessing whether those distinctions constitute genuine novelty or overstatement is load-bearing if 'What do you think?' targets scientific merit."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Are you asking for an evaluation of scientific and methodological soundness—whether the results support the 'state-of-the-art' claim and whether RDL is genuinely novel—rather than for feedback on writing quality and presentation?"}

rationale.R1.1: The section advances a specific contribution claim (RDL, multi-task formulation) and a gap statement (majority-vote bias); a general opinion request plausibly invites evaluation of whether that framing is accurate and well-scoped.
rationale.R1.2: The section builds a causal chain—non-negligible reader disagreement → majority-vote bias → RDL remedy → improved AUC and ECE—whose internal logical coherence is a natural target of any open-ended critical opinion.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative performance claims are asserted (AUC 0.872, ECE 0.039 vs. baselines on CSAW-CC) against a training set (DDSM-Plus) different from the evaluation set; evidence-claim alignment is central to assessing whether those numbers actually support the stated conclusions.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly differentiates RDL from symmetric label smoothing and prior disagreement-aware methods; assessing whether those distinctions constitute genuine novelty or overstatement is load-bearing if 'What do you think?' targets scientific merit.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for an evaluation of scientific and methodological soundness—whether the results support the 'state-of-the-art' claim and whether RDL is genuinely novel—rather than for feedback on writing quality and presentation?
